title: LeViT and ConViT
concept: LeViT and ConViT are optimized hybrid models designed for fast edge-device inference by combining lightweight convolutions with gated attention mechanisms.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is the main design advantage of placing convolutions early and attention late?

options.text: It handles high-resolution features locally and avoids running self-attention on long sequences.
It removes the need for residual skip connections.
It doubles the parameters of the MLP head.

exercises.explain: Correct! Early convolutions downsample the spatial dimensions, keeping the late attention sequence short.

cards.title: Transformers on your phone
cards.body: A standard Vision Transformer is too heavy to run smoothly on a smartwatch or smartphone. To bring transformer power to edge devices, researchers designed special speed-optimized hybrids like LeViT and ConViT. Let's see how they achieved speed without losing accuracy.

cards.title: LeViT and ConViT
cards.body: LeViT is a hybrid model that uses a fast convolutional pyramid to downsample images quickly, combined with attention blocks that use linear projections instead of expensive dot-products. ConViT uses a gated self-attention mechanism that mimics convolutions, letting the model decide whether to look locally or globally.

cards.title: A hybrid commuter bike
cards.body: Imagine a heavy mountain bike (ViT) and a fragile road racer (CNN). LeViT is like a hybrid commuter bike: it has a light frame (convolutions) to handle speed bumps and narrow paths, but has gears (attention) to zoom down open straights. It's built specifically for efficient daily travel.

cards.title: Speed benchmarks
cards.body: On standard mobile processors, LeViT-256 runs up to **5 times faster** than standard ViT-Base, while achieving similar accuracy on ImageNet, making it a viable option for real-time mobile app deployment.

cards.title: Not just scaling down parameters
cards.body: You might think LeViT is fast simply because it is small. In reality, it uses structural changes: it removes the CLS token in favor of average pooling, uses batch normalization instead of layer normalization, and replaces position embeddings with attention biases.

left.text:
LayerNorm throughout
Position embeddings at start
Heavy computation; slow on edge

right.text:
BatchNorm for faster speed
Relative attention bias positional info
Lightweight; designed for edge devices

exercises.prompt: What is the primary design goal of the LeViT architecture?
exercises.explain: LeViT combines fast convolutional downsampling with optimized attention blocks to speed up mobile and edge inference.

options.text: To achieve high-speed, lightweight transformer inference on edge devices
To increase the depth of the network to 1000 layers
To train without using any backward propagation
To eliminate the use of color channels in the input

exercises.prompt: True or false: LeViT uses Layer Normalization just like standard Vision Transformers.
exercises.explain: False. LeViT replaces Layer Normalization with Batch Normalization because BN can be fused with adjacent convs during inference, saving compute.
Global average pooling computes the mean vector of features, which is faster than routing an extra CLS token.
exercises.sentence: To optimize speed, LeViT replaces the standard CLS token with global average ___ before the classifier.

exercises.prompt: Which efficiency feature is found in LeViT to replace standard absolute position embeddings?
exercises.explain: LeViT uses relative position biases within the attention calculation, which is more robust to changes in resolution.

options.text: Relative position bias injected directly into the attention blocks
Removing all spatial position tracking completely

exercises.prompt: How does ConViT's gated self-attention mechanism behave during the start of training?

options.text: It uses a gating parameter initialized to mimic local convolutions, which the model can gradually open up to capture global relationships as it learns.
It randomly deletes half the attention heads in each epoch.
It forces all tokens to look only at the first patch.
It locks the attention weights to prevent them from updating.

exercises.explain: Incredible! ConViT initializes attention to behave like local convolutions, then lets backpropagation slowly expand the attention field as training progresses. Next: convolutional projection within attention blocks!
